person.name: Owain ap Gruffydd, of Deheubarth
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Joint heir with Rhys Ieuanc of Gruffydd, eldest son of the 'lord' Rhys. His mother was Matilda, daughter of William de Breos. Though at times temporarily in opposition to Llywelyn ap Iorwerth, he and his brother found in the prince of Gwynedd a powerful patron and defender against their uncles - Rhys Gryg and Maelgwn. Originally endowed with land in Cantref Bychan, the re-division of the 'lord' Rhys's dominions in 1216 gave him lands north of the Aeron, which were extended to cover the greater part of northern Ceredigion when Rhys Ieuanc died in 1222. He died at Strata Florida on 18 January 1236 and was buried there alongside his parents and brother. He was succeeded by one son - Maredudd.
https://biography.wales/article/s-OWAI-APG-1236

Owain ap Gruffydd (died 18 January 1236 Strata Florida Abbey) was a lord of the Welsh principality of Deheubarth.

Owain ap Gruffydd was of the Dinefwr dynasty. He was a son of Gruffydd ap Rhys and his wife Matilda de Braose, a daughter of William de Braose. Along with his brother Rhys Ieuanc, on his father's death in 1201, he inherited his lands and claims to the inheritance of their grandfather Rhys ap Gruffydd, Lord of Deheubarth. Their inheritance was contested by their uncle Maelgwn ap Rhys. After Maelgwn had usurped the Ystrad Tywi in 1203, he was in 1204 by Owain ap Gruffydd, Rhys Ieuanc and their uncle Rhys Grygexpelled again. In the ensuing battles for Deheubarth, Owain and his brother Rhys Ieuanc always seemed to be on the wrong side. In 1211 Owain supported Prince Llywelyn ap Iorwerth of Gwynedd in the war against the English King John Lackland, which brought him into conflict with Rhys Gryg, who conquered Llandovery Castle in 1211. In 1212, however, Owain, along with his brother Rhys Ieuanc, was back on the side of King John, who sent an army under the mercenary leader Falkes de Bréauté to assist them. This defeated Rhys Gryg at Llandeilo in January 1213, so that his lands fell to his nephews. In the spring of 1215, Owain and Rhys Ieuanc allied again with Maelgwn ap Rhys against the king and supported Llywelyn from Iorwerth in North Wales.

Originally endowed with lands in the Cantref Bychan, Owain was granted lands north of the River Aeron in Ceredigion in 1216 in the Treaty of Aberdyfi, which regulated the partition of Deheubarth. For this, like the other lords of Deheubarth, he had to recognize the suzerainty of Llywelyn ap Iorwerth and render aid to him in arms. As early as 1217 Owain fought alongside Llywelyn from Iorwerth against the Anglo-Norman baron Reginald de Braose, a brother of his mother.

Given the brutal battles of succession within their family, Owain's longstanding alliance with his brother Rhys Ieuanc is notable. Their close collaboration was perhaps encouraged by their origins, as children of an Anglo-Norman mother they were considered outsiders within the family. After the death of Rhys Ieuanc in August 1222, however, Owain was unable to completely take over his territory in the north of Ceredigion, but had to share it with Maelgwn ap Rhys. Owain died at Strata Florida Abbey and was buried there alongside his parents and brother. His heir and successor was his son Maredudd.
https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Owain_ap_Gruffydd_(%E2%80%A0_1236)

zh.lifeSummary: 當Owain ap Gruffydd, of Deheubarth於1185年出生在威爾士卡馬特史爾丁萊 卡素時，他的父親Gruffydd II ap Rhys28歲，母親Matilda de Braose13歲。他和Angharad verch Gwgan Sais至少有3個兒子。他去世於1236年1月18日在威爾士卡迪甘史爾斯特拉塔 福洛利達享年51歲，埋葬在威爾士卡迪甘史爾特雷加隆。

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Long, Minh, Muoi, Phan, Phat, Vinh, Cuong, Hien, Hoa, Lam, Lan.  Chinese Wing, Chi, Kin, Cheuk, Chi Wai, Chuen, Hon, Keung, Wah, Wai, Chiu, Ho.
Chinese: alternative Mandarin form of the surname 敖, see   Ao  .
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Auerbach
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Moshe, Emanuel, Isidor, Bronia, Chaim, Faigy, Menachem, Meyer, Yechezkel.
German: habitational name from any of several places in southern Germany and Austria called Auerbach ‘the stream at the water meadow’. See also 2,   Au  , and   Bach  .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from the town of Auerbach in the Upper Palatinate, Bavaria. Compare   Averbach  .

variants.name: Ao
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 敖, meaning both ‘rambling’ and ‘deposed king without posthumous titles’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the personal name of Tai Ao (太敖), teacher of the legendary king Zhuanxu (traditional dates: 2514–2437 BC ). (ii) it is said that the surname is also borne by descendants of deposed kings of the former state of Chu, with no posthumous titles, during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

variants.name: Auer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Alois, Hans, Erwin, Matthias, Siegfried, Bernd, Bernhard, Erna, Franz, Fritz, Hermann, Horst.

German (mainly Bavaria): topographic name for someone living by a water meadow, German Au, or a habitational name for someone from a place called Au or Aue (see Au and Aue ). This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Czechia, and Slovenia.

nameMeanings.name: Gruffydd,
meanings: Welsh: The Welsh name; from creffydd, strong faith, or strong in faith. Gruffydd, a Welsh form of Gryffydd.

variants.name: Gruffydd
meanings: Welsh: The Welsh name; from creffydd, strong faith, or strong in faith. Gruffydd, a Welsh form of Gryffydd.

nameMeanings.name: Owain
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Perhaps derived in the Roman period from the Latin name Eugenius ( see Eugene ); alternatively, possibly from an Old Celtic name meaning ‘born of Esos’. Esos (or Aesos) was a Celtic god known to have been the subject of a cult in ancient Gaul.

en.lifeSummary: When Owain ap Gruffydd, of Deheubarth was born in 1185, in Dinefwr Castle, Carmarthenshire, Wales, his father, Gruffydd II ap Rhys, was 28 and his mother, Matilda de Braose, was 13. He had at least 3 sons with Angharad verch Gwgan Sais. He died on 18 January 1236, in Strata Florida, Cardiganshire, Wales, at the age of 51, and was buried in Tregaron, Cardiganshire, Wales.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Owain ap Gruffydd, of Deheubarth est né en 1185, à Dinefwr Castle, Carmarthenshire, Galles, son père, Gruffydd II ap Rhys, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Matilda de Braose, avait 13 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils avec Angharad verch Gwgan Sais. Il est décédé le 18 janvier 1236, à Strata Florida, Cardiganshire, Galles, à l’âge de 51 ans, et a été enterré à Tregaron, Cardiganshire, Galles.

de.lifeSummary: Als Owain ap Gruffydd, of Deheubarth 1185 in Dinefwr Castle, Carmarthenshire, Wales geboren wurde, war sein Vater Gruffydd II ap Rhys 28 und seine Mutter Matilda de Braose 13. Er hatte mit Angharad verch Gwgan Sais mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb am 18. Januar 1236 im Alter von 51 Jahren in Strata Florida, Cardiganshire, Wales und wurde in Tregaron, Cardiganshire, Wales begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Owain ap Gruffydd, of Deheubarth è nato il nel 1185 nel Dinefwr Castle, Carmarthenshire, Galles, il suo padre, Gruffydd II ap Rhys, aveva 28 anni e il suo madre, Matilda de Braose, aveva 13 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli con Angharad verch Gwgan Sais. Morì il 18 gennaio 1236, nel Strata Florida, Cardiganshire, Galles, all'età di 51 anni, e fu sepolto a Tregaron, Cardiganshire, Galles.

ja.lifeSummary: Owain ap Gruffydd, of Deheubarthが1185年にウェールズカーマースエンシュアイアディンエフウル カストルで生まれた時，父Gruffydd II ap Rhysは28歳，母Matilda de Braoseは13歳であった。彼はAngharad verch Gwgan Saisとの間に少なくとも3人の息子をもうけた。彼は1236年1月18日にウェールズカーディガンシュアイアストラタ フロリダで51歳で死去し，ウェールズカーディガンシュアイアトレガーオンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Owain ap Gruffydd, of Deheubarth(이)가 1185년 웨일스 카마테실르 딘프르 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gruffydd II ap Rhys(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Matilda de Braose(은)는 13세였다. 그는 Angharad verch Gwgan Sais(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1236년 1월 18일 웨일스 카디건실르 스트라타 플로리다에서 51세의 나이로 사망했으며 웨일스 카디건실르 트레가에 묻혔다.

mg.Deheubarth, Carmarthenshire, Wales: Carmarthenshire, Pays de Galles, United Kingdom

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Owain ap Gruffydd, of Deheubarth nasceu em 1185, em Dinefwr Castle, Carmarthenshire, Gales, seu pai, Gruffydd II ap Rhys, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Matilda de Braose, tinha 13 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos com Angharad verch Gwgan Sais. Ele faleceu em 18 de janeiro de 1236, em Strata Florida, Cardiganshire, Gales, com 51 anos, e foi sepultado em Tregaron, Cardiganshire, Gales.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Owain ap Gruffydd, of Deheubarth родился в 1185 г. в местности даинофр кастл, кармартеншир, Уэльс, его отцу по имени Gruffydd II ap Rhys было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Matilda de Braose, 13. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына от Angharad verch Gwgan Sais. Он умер 18 января 1236 г. в местности страта флорида, кардиганьшир, Уэльс в возрасте 51 год и был похоронен в местности трегарон, кардиганьшир, Уэльс.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Owain ap Gruffydd, of Deheubarth nació en 1185, en Dinefwr Castle, Carmarthenshire, Gales, su papá, Gruffydd II ap Rhys, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Matilda de Braose, tenía 13 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos con Angharad verch Gwgan Sais. Murió el 18 de enero de 1236, en Strata Florida, Cardiganshire, Gales, a la edad de 51 años, y fue sepultado en Tregaron, Cardiganshire, Gales.
